perturbation.original_text: SECTION 3. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 3. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3 and Section 7) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "and Section 7" to Section 3 creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3 states that Confidential Information shall be kept confidential and not disclosed, but the added reference to Section 7, which states 'Neither party hereto shall be under any obligation of any kind with respect to the Transaction, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein, unless and until a definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed and delivered by each of the parties hereto,' implies that Section 7 contains exceptions to non-disclosure. However, Section 7 deals with liability and obligations regarding the transaction itself, not the Confidential Information, thus the change creates an unclear implication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 3. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3 and Section 7) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3 states that confidential information shall be kept confidential and not disclosed to any third party, except as provided in Section 3. The changed text modifies this by adding 'and Section 7', implying that Section 7 contains exceptions to non-disclosure. However, the explanation states that Section 7 deals with liability and obligations regarding the transaction itself, not the Confidential Information. Therefore, the addition of 'and Section 7' creates an in-text contradiction because it implies an exception to non-disclosure in a section that doesn't actually provide such an exception, making the obligation of non-disclosure ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, except those retained in accordance with Section 8, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof, unless otherwise stated in Section 8.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4 mandates the return of *all* documents containing Confidential Information. By adding 'except those retained in accordance with Section 8' and 'unless otherwise stated in Section 8', we introduce a contradiction. Section 8, which is about non-solicitation of employees, has nothing to do with retaining confidential information. This change suggests an exception to the return policy exists within Section 8, creating an in-text contradiction as Section 8 does not address document retention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, except those retained in accordance with Section 8, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof, unless otherwise stated in Section 8.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4 mandates the return of *all* documents containing Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception by adding 'except those retained in accordance with Section 8' and 'unless otherwise stated in Section 8'. According to the provided explanation, Section 8 is about non-solicitation of employees, and has nothing to do with retaining confidential information. Therefore, the change introduces an exception that is not supported by the content of Section 8. This creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction in Section 4, because it suggests the possibility of retaining documents under a condition that Section 8 doesn't even address.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 5. NO LICENSES. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 5. NO LICENSES. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as provided in Section 6.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that no license or right to use Confidential Information is granted, except as *otherwise provided herein*. This implies that there may be other sections within the document which outline permitted uses. By changing this to 'except as provided in Section 6' we create a contradiction. Section 6 deals with non-disclosure of the transaction, not the use of the confidential information, so there are no clauses in section 6 about the use of the confidential information, making the exception misleading and contradictory to the general intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 5. NO LICENSES. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as provided in Section 6.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for exceptions to the 'no license' clause as 'otherwise provided herein.' The changed text narrows this exception to 'as provided in Section 6.' However, Section 6 doesn't address the use of confidential information. This creates a contradiction because the general prohibition on using confidential information has an exception that doesn't actually allow for any use, despite implying that it does, creating a contradiction with the intention of the section itself.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 8. NON-SOLICITATION. The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other or any of its Affiliates on the date of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 8. NON-SOLICITATION. The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other or any of its Affiliates on the date of this agreement, except as outlined in Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except as outlined in Section 2' to the end of Section 8, which is about non-solicitation, introduces a direct in-text contradiction. Section 2 is the Ownership clause. There is nothing related to employee solicitation. This statement creates an in-text contradiction because the non-solicitation agreement is clearly stated, but then implies there are exceptions within a section unrelated to employment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 8. NON-SOLICITATION. The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other or any of its Affiliates on the date of this agreement, except as outlined in Section 2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'except as outlined in Section 2' to the non-solicitation agreement in Section 8. Section 2 is about Ownership and unrelated to employee solicitation. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 8 itself, as it initially prohibits solicitation and then implies exceptions within an irrelevant section. This conflicts with the contradicted text because it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty into a previously clear non-solicitation clause, rendering the agreement's enforceability questionable.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 11. TERM. Except as specified elsewhere herein, the term of this agreement shall be six months commencing on the date hereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 11. TERM. Except as specified in Section 1, the term of this agreement shall be six months commencing on the date hereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 11, concerning the term of the agreement, allows for exceptions specified *elsewhere herein*. By modifying this to 'Except as specified in Section 1', the contract now specifically points only to Section 1 for any variations to the term. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information' and does not address the term of the agreement, creating a clear contradiction. This is a case where a perturbation is made, and it contradicts itself, because there is no place where the term has changed in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 11. TERM. Except as specified in Section 1, the term of this agreement shall be six months commencing on the date hereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 11 allows for exceptions to the term 'elsewhere herein'. The changed Section 11 now restricts exceptions only to Section 1. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information', not the term of the agreement. The changed section contradicts itself, according to the explanation. The contradicted text is the changed text itself. The change creates a clear contradiction.
